pi_items.source: GitHub release tag at https://github.com/OpenBCI/OpenBCI_GUI/releases/tag/v5.2.2 (released August 21, 2023). Installation script downloads from: https://github.com/OpenBCI/OpenBCI_GUI/releases/download/v5.2.2/openbcigui_v5.2.2_2023-08-21_16-14-34_linux64.zip
Task setup_task.sh and task description - task requires loading and playing back a recorded EEG file

File header declares: '%Source = PhysioNet EEG Motor Movement/Imagery Dataset (eegmmidb 1.0.0)'. Installation script downloads from https://physionet.org/files/eegmmidb/1.0.0/S001/S001R01.edf if needed. PhysioNet dataset is real, publicly available with open access: https://physionet.org/content/eegmmidb/1.0.0/

pi_items.metadata_value: Subject S001, Run R01 (eyes open baseline)
pi_items.verified_value: Subject S001, Run R01 (eyes open baseline)
pi_items.source: File header declares: '%Subject = S001'. PhysioNet dataset structure uses S001R01 (Subject 1, Run 1) which is designated as baseline with eyes open. Task description and multiple task READMEs confirm this is the eyes-open baseline.
File header declares: '%Number of channels = 8'. Confirmed by parsing file header and data column count (8 EXG channels: Fp1, Fp2, C3, C4, P7, P8, O1, O2)
File header declares: '%Sample Rate = 250 Hz'. Verified by examining timestamp intervals: samples are spaced 4ms apart (0.004 seconds), which equals 250 Hz (1/0.004 = 250)

File analysis: 15,258 total lines minus 8 header lines = 15,250 data samples. At 250 Hz sample rate: 15,250 / 250 = 61 seconds exactly. Confirmed by last timestamp in file (2024-01-15 10:01:01.000 = 61 seconds after start of 2024-01-15 10:00:00.000)

Task requires navigating OpenBCI GUI menus, selecting playback mode, locating file, and starting session. Related task README (eyes_open_alpha_analysis) rates similar tasks as 'very hard' due to multiple required interactions, suggesting 'medium' is a reasonable conservative assessment for the simpler load_playback_recording task.

privileged_info_summary: The OpenBCI-EEG-S001-EyesOpen.txt file contains real EEG data derived from PhysioNet's public EEG Motor Movement/Imagery Database (eegmmidb 1.0.0), Subject S001 Run R01 (eyes-open baseline). The data comprises 8 channels (Fp1, Fp2, C3, C4, P7, P8, O1, O2) sampled at 250 Hz for exactly 61 seconds. The recording was converted from the original PhysioNet EDF format (64 channels, 160 Hz) via resampling and channel selection to match OpenBCI GUI requirements. OpenBCI GUI v5.2.2 (August 2023 release) is a legitimate, publicly available application from GitHub used for EEG visualization and analysis.
